description: Google's standard L3 interview loop — 6 rounds covering coding, system design, and behavioral skills

rounds.name: Online Assessment
rounds.description: Two medium-hard coding problems, timed. Tests your problem-solving speed and ability to write clean code under pressure.

rounds.name: Phone Screen
rounds.description: One coding problem with a Google engineer observing your thought process. Think aloud and communicate your approach.

rounds.name: Onsite: Coding 1
rounds.description: Medium-hard algorithm problem. Interviewers value communication and trade-off discussion as much as correct code.

rounds.name: Onsite: Coding 2
rounds.description: Hard algorithm problem. Multiple approaches expected — start with brute force then optimize.

rounds.name: Onsite: System Design
rounds.description: Design a large-scale distributed system. Focus on scalability, reliability, and trade-offs between architectural choices.

rounds.name: Onsite: Googleyness
rounds.description: Assesses collaboration, intellectual humility, comfort with ambiguity, and ability to get things done.
